slides.headline: We design and build world-class digital products.
slides.body: A partnership built on craft, speed, and shared ambition.

slides.label: O ponto de viragem
slides.headline: O site nao esta velho. Esta a tornar-vos invisiveis.

slides.label: A abordagem
slides.headline: Começamos pelo que vos torna invisíveis.
slides.lede: Antes de desenhar seja o que for, percebemos porque é que o que está hoje não está a funcionar.
slides.body: Mapeamos onde a procura existe e onde ainda não estais presentes. Cruzamos dados de pesquisa com o comportamento real dos utilizadores para perceber onde o interesse aparece e onde se perde. Identificamos o que a concorrência está a fazer bem, o que está a ignorar, e onde os utilizadores chegam ao vosso site e saem sem converter. Este mapeamento revela as lacunas que um redesenho bem dirigido consegue fechar. Só depois deste trabalho é que sabemos o que construir e porquê. A diferença entre um site que converte e um que não converte raramente está no design. Está no entendimento de quem chega, o que procura, e o que o faz ficar.
slides.after: Esta análise é o mapa que guia todas as decisões seguintes -- do que se mostra em primeiro lugar ao que se mantém fora do caminho crítico.

slides.label: The craft behind the product
slides.headline: We work in the open, at every stage.
slides.body: Every sprint ends with something real to look at. No status reports, no intermediary layers -- just the product, live, in your browser.

slides.label: Product in motion
slides.headline: The full flow, from first click to checkout.
slides.body: Recorded on the working prototype. Every interaction you see is real -- no mocks, no smoke and mirrors.

slides.label: Our commitments
slides.headline: What you can count on.
slides.body: A fixed-scope engagement with no surprises. Every deliverable is defined upfront and signed off before work begins.

items.heading: A single point of contact
items.body: One product lead accountable for delivery from kickoff to handoff. No account managers, no delegation chains.

items.heading: Weekly progress demos
items.body: Every sprint ends with a live demo. You see real, working product — not status updates in a slide.

items.heading: No surprises on scope
items.body: Scope is agreed in writing before work begins. Any change is flagged before it affects the timeline or budget.

items.heading: Post-launch support included
items.body: We remain on standby for four weeks after launch to address anything that comes up in the real world.

slides.label: Industry failure rate
slides.headline: Most digital products don't survive.
slides.body: Only 4 in 100 digital products launched in the last decade are still in active use today.
slides.valueTitle: Still in active use after 2 years
slides.caption: The industry's default outcome is abandonment.

slides.label: Content fits inside — ratio 65/100
slides.headline: Both bars tall enough to hold label inside.
slides.body: Smaller bar is 65% of the taller — above the 40% threshold, so both labels render inside the fill.

slides.label: Content above bar — ratio 20/100
slides.headline: Short bar pushes label above.
slides.body: Smaller bar is 20% of the taller — below the 40% threshold, so the label moves above the fill in black.

tabs.label: Discovery
body: We start every engagement by building a shared understanding of the problem. Through structured workshops, stakeholder interviews and a technical audit, we define the product vision, identify constraints and align on what success looks like.
This phase is the foundation everything else is built on. Getting it right means the rest of the project moves faster, with fewer surprises and a team that is genuinely aligned on what matters.

deliverables.label: Stakeholder interviews
deliverables.description: Structured sessions to align on goals and constraints.

deliverables.label: Technical audit
deliverables.description: Assessment of existing systems and integration requirements.

deliverables.label: Product roadmap
deliverables.description: Prioritised feature list with success metrics defined.

team.description: Facilitates workshops and defines the product roadmap.

tabs.label: Design
body: Design is where the product takes shape. We move from rough concepts to a fully resolved interface — information architecture, interaction design, visual language and a component system that scales. Every decision is grounded in user needs and validated through iteration.

You will see and respond to real work throughout, not at the end. We share progress weekly, incorporate feedback continuously, and make sure nothing ships that has not been reviewed and approved by your team.

deliverables.label: Information architecture
deliverables.description: Sitemap, user flows and navigation structure.

deliverables.label: High-fidelity UI
deliverables.description: Fully resolved screens for all core flows.

deliverables.label: Design system
deliverables.description: Component library with tokens, ready for handoff.

deliverables.label: Prototype
deliverables.description: Interactive clickable prototype for stakeholder review.

team.description: Leads visual design, UI and the design system.
Owns information architecture and interaction design.

tabs.label: Development
body: We build in short cycles, shipping working software every week. Design and engineering operate in the same loop — no handoff, no silos. The codebase is production-ready from day one: tested, documented and built to last.
We integrate with your existing systems and keep you in the loop at every step. Every sprint ends with a demo, and every decision is documented so you always understand what was built and why.

deliverables.label: Frontend
deliverables.description: Production-ready UI built against the design system.

deliverables.label: Backend & API
deliverables.description: Server-side logic, database and third-party integrations.

deliverables.label: QA & testing
deliverables.description: End-to-end test suite covering critical paths.

team.description: Builds the UI layer and integrates with APIs.
Architects and implements the server-side logic.
Defines test plans and ensures quality at every step.

tabs.label: Launch
body: Launch is not the end — it is the beginning of the product's life. We manage the full deployment, set up monitoring and alerts, and make sure your team is confident operating what we have built together.

After go-live, we remain available for a period of post-launch support to address anything that comes up. We treat launch not as the end of the engagement but as the start of the product's life in the real world.

deliverables.label: Deployment
deliverables.description: Production environment setup with CI/CD pipeline.

deliverables.label: Monitoring
deliverables.description: Error tracking, uptime alerts and performance dashboards.

deliverables.label: Documentation
deliverables.description: Technical and user-facing documentation for the team.

team.description: Coordinates handoff, training and post-launch support.

slides.label: The real odds
slides.headline: Most digital products don't survive.
slides.body: Building something is the easy part. Most products ship, get used briefly, and quietly disappear. The ones that don't are built differently — with more rigour, sharper focus, and the right partner from day one.
